Ad Text:
Lead Business Analyst – Automation & AI

Lead innovation. Drive real impact. 
Collaborative, positive culture with strong team support 
Permanent role | Melbourne-based | 3 Days in office & 2 days WFH

This is an opportunity to join a team driving genuine, enterprise-wide transformation.

Role Details:

You will work at the intersection of strategy, operations, and technology, helping Visy unlock value from automation, RPA and AI across multiple business units. The role has strong executive visibility and direct impact on our digital roadmap and operational efficiency.

You will accelerate our digital and AI transformation agenda across the business. With a growing portfolio of automation and AI initiatives spanning Finance, Logistics, Procurement, Shared Services and other core functions, you will lead the expansion of our AI Centre of Excellence.

You will be a critical enabler of our transformation strategy, identifying opportunities, shaping business cases, designing future‑state processes, and ensuring AI and automation initiatives deliver measurable business value.

What you’ll do:

In this strategic and hands-on role, you will:

Lead structured process discovery workshops to identify RPA, automation, AI and GenAI opportunities across business units.
Conduct end-to-end assessments (As‑Is / To‑Be, feasibility, value and ROI analysis) for automation and AI use cases.
Define and document business requirements, user stories and future‑state process models aligned to Visy’s digital strategy.
Manage and prioritise the automation & AI pipeline, ensuring clear intake, triage, governance and tracking.
Partner closely with technical and delivery teams to shape solution scope and support successful implementation.
Influence and manage stakeholders across business and IT, supporting change management and adoption.

What you bring:

We’re looking for a strategic Lead BA who can operate comfortably in both detail and big picture:

10–12 years’ experience in business analysis, transformation or process re‑engineering roles.
Significant experience across automation / RPA / AI / digital transformation programs in complex environments.
Strong track record in process mapping (As‑Is / To‑Be), opportunity qualification and business case development.
Proven stakeholder management skills, including working with senior business and technology leaders.
Excellent facilitation, documentation and presentation skills, with the ability to produce executive‑ready materials.
Experience with tools such as RPA platforms and AI / GenAI technologies (e.g. Microsoft Copilot, Azure OpenAI) is highly regarded.

 What you’ll love when you join us:  

Competitive salary and benefits
High‑impact digital transformation work
Supportive, collaborative team
Strong career growth opportunities
Lifestyle and purchased leave options
Discounts with RSEA, Harvey Norman, BMW, Avis and more

Ready to make a difference? Apply now and be a key player in shaping our future!  

About Visy

Visy is powering the circular economy, closing the loop between packaging, remanufacturing and recycling.  Over 75 years, we've grown to 7,000 employees and 150 global operations – predominantly in Australia and New Zealand.  

We believe materials are not recycled until they’ve been made into new products. We collect and sort recyclable materials like paper, cardboard, glass, plastics and metals from households and businesses, and locally remanufacture them back into new, sustainable packaging. From cardboard boxes, water bottles and jam jars to food and beverage cans, we are using recycled content to power Australia and New Zealand’s food and beverage packaging industry. 

Ready to make a difference? Apply now and be a key player in shaping our future!  

You will be required to meet Visy’s employment criteria, which will include a criminal history check.   

Visy is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing a working environment that embraces and values diversity and inclusion.

Ad Text:
What you'll do  

Undertake strategic analysis of key documents and undertake research to provide high quality advice and support to senior health executives on national digital health and information initiatives by developing briefs, speaking notes and other comments in the corporate format required.
Review the work program activities for national digital health, information and standards organisations—i.e. the Australian Digital Health Agency—for corporate endorsement and provide advice regarding their alignment with the Queensland Health and Hospital and Health Services positioning, capabilities and other national digital health and information programs.
Coordinate high quality responses to internal and external requests by engaging key stakeholders to ensure consistency and alignment with Queensland Health priorities.
Support eHealth Queensland to capitalise on strategic partnerships by supporting visibility of partnerships and their progress.
Develop and maintain effective networks by consulting with internal and external clients, interest groups, other government agencies and other stakeholders.

About you  

We are searching for someone who has:

Undertake research and high level strategic analysis of issues being considered by national committees and departmental committees in relation to the national digital health agenda.
Highly developed written and oral communication skills, with the proven ability to provide high quality advice on a range of issues and to prepare submissions, briefings and reports.
Proven ability to work autonomously, manage and plan personal work activities, utilise discretion, meet deadlines, establish priorities and work as an effective member of a team to successfully contribute to organisational objectives, with the flexibility to adapt to emerging priorities.
Highly developed interpersonal, presentation and consultation skills, with proven ability to build and maintain effective relationships with senior and multi-disciplinary staff at the organisational, state, national, sector and industry levels.
High level understanding of the application of digital health and ICT strategies, policies and standards and their contribution to effective health service delivery in Queensland and at a national level.

Why work with us?  

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. 

Competitive salaries 

12.75% superannuation  
17.5% leave loading 

Employee wellbeing & development 

Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members 
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave. 
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.  
Grow your skills through hands-on experience and access to internal training opportunities and additional financial and leave benefits for approved external training and development. 

Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion  

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.  

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions.

For further information on how to apply please review the attached Role Description.

Valrose is collaborating with a key client to find a Process Analyst to analyse, map, and identify process improvement opportunities across an employee journey lifecycle. 

What would be considered a successful contract engagement 

Within the contract timeframe, you would have:

Documented current HR processes
Completed and advanced pending process tasks
Mapped the employee journey clearly enough to guide future process design.
Your work will provide the business with:

Greater visibility of its existing processes
A clearer understanding of how these processes impact the end-user experience
A strong basis for the upcoming stage of transformation.
What you will bring to the role

Your work in process analysis, journey mapping, and business improvement laid the groundwork for successful HR systems transformation programs.
Your background in experience-led service design has led to higher satisfaction with the employee journey.
Your extensive expertise in HR transformation guides your pragmatic method for spotting process issues and opportunities for improvement.

This is a genuine chance to add value quickly through a visible transformation project. The client seeks someone who can focus on the process and people aspects of the work, rather than purely technical skills. If you enjoy simplifying complexity, making processes more user-friendly, and connecting operational design to real end-user experience, this opportunity is a strong match.
